content.full_text:
January 28, 1986


Victor J. Westman
County Counsel
Contra Costa County
P.O. Box 69
Martinez, CA 94553-0006

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-86-031

Dear Mr. Westman:

You have written requesting our advice regarding your filing obligations under the recently revised provisions of Government Code Section 87200, et seq., amended by Chapter 611, Stats. 1985. Your understanding of your requirements is correct. For calendar year 1985, you will continue to file a Form 730 pursuant to your agency's Conflict of Interest Code. For calendar year, 1986, you will file pursuant to the requirements of Section 87200, et seq. This means that your Statement of Economic Interests for 1986 will be filed with our office by the County Clerk after you have filed the Form 721 with that office. That statement will be due in the Spring of 1987.

I trust that this letter adequately responds to your request for advice. Should you have any questions regarding this letter, I may be reached at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,


Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel
Legal Division

REL:plh


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 19, 1985


TO:     CITY ATTORNEYS, COUNTY COUNSELS AND DISTRICT ATTORNEYS

FROM:   TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE AND ANALYSIS DIVISION

RE:     STATEMENTS OF ECONOMIC INTERESTS


     Enclosed for your information are the 1985-86 Statements of
Economic Interests (Form 721 and Form 730), Instruction Manuals
and newsletter to filing officials and filing officers. City
Clerks, County Clerks and Registrars of Voters were recently
mailed these forms and manuals for distribution to the public
officials specified in Government Code Section 87200 and to the
designated employees specified in the city's or county's conflict
of interest code.

     As you may know, the Political Reform Act was amended (1985
Stats. Chapter 611, A.B. 670), effective January 1, 1986, to
include city attorneys and county counsels as officials covered
by Government Code Section 87200 who are required to file the 721
Form. (Currently, city attorneys and county counsels are
required to file Statements of Economic Interests, Form 730, only
if they are designated in the city's or county's conflict of
interest code).

     Therefore, if you leave office during 1986 you must file a
Form 721 for your leaving office Statement of Economic Interests
within 30 days of leaving office. In addition, city attorneys
and county counsels who assume office during 1986 must file a
Form 721 as an assuming office Statement of Economic Interests
within 30 days of assuming office. Your first annual 721 Form
will be due April 1, 1987. This Statement will cover the period
from January 1, 1986 through December 31, 1986.

     If you are currently covered by a local conflict of interest
code, you must file your annual Statement covering 1985 on the
730 Form pursuant to the conflict of interest code.

     If you have any questions, please call us at (916) 322-5662.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

VICTOR J. WESTMAN
COUNTY COUNSEL

SILVANO B. MARCHESI
ARTHUR P. VALENCIA, JR.
ASSISTANTS

MICHAEL D. FARR
EDWARD V. LANE, JR.
PRINCIPAL DEPUTIES

OFFICE OF COUNTY COUNSEL

CONTRA COSTA COUNTY
COUNTY ADMINISTRATION BUILDING

P.O. BOX 671
MARTINEZ, CALIFORNIA 94553-0006
PHONE: (415) 372-2074

DEPUTIES
SHARON L. ANDERSON
KAREN M. CLAYTON
VICKIE L. DAWES
VICKI J. FINUCANE
DOLORES A. FINE
DENNIS C. GRAVES
ELIZABETH B. HEAREY
KEVIN T. KERR
PAUL R MUNIZ
MARGARET O. NEIL
DAVID F. SCHMIDT
LORRAINE M. WALSH

January 10, 1986

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento CA 95804

Re: County Counsel filing, Chap. 611 (AB 670)

Dear Commission Staff:

In 1984 I assumed a four-year term of office as Contra
Costa County's County Counsel. I do not anticipate leaving
office during 1986.

Under the provisions of Chapter 611 of the Statutes of
1995, I believe that I am not required to file an "Assumed
Office Statement of Economic Interest" or "Annual Statement"
during 1986. If I correctly understand the note on page 2 of
your "1985-86 Manual...for Form 721," the first filing required
of me with your Commission will be a "First Annual Statement"
due April 1, 1987 covering calendar year 1986. Of course, I
will file in Martinez (for calendar year 1985) a disclosure
statement pursuant to Contra Costa County's adopted conflict
of interest regulations.

I would appreciate hearing whether you agree or dis-
agree with my understanding of Chapter 611. Your assistance
in this regard, will be greatly appreciated.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Victor J. Westman
County Counsel

VJW/jh
cc: County Elections Dept.
    Attn: Robert J. Delevati

sections.question: You have written requesting our advice regarding your filing obligations under the recently revised provisions of Government code section 87200, et seg., amended by Chapter 611, Stats. 1985. Specifically, whether as County Counsel assuming a four-year term in 1984 and not anticipating leaving office during 1986, you are required to file an "Assuming Office Statement of Economic Interest" or "Annual Statement" during 1986, and when your first filing with the Commission will be due.
sections.conclusion: Your understanding of your requirements is correct. For calendar year 1985, you will continue to file a Form 730 pursuant to your agency's Conflict of Interest Code. For calendar year 1986, you will file pursuant to the requirements of Section 87200, et seg. This means that your Statement of Economic Interests for 1986 will be filed with our office by the county Clerk after you have filed the Form 721 with that office. That statement will be due in the Spring of 1987.
sections.question_synthetic: What are the filing obligations for a County Counsel under the newly amended Government Code section 87200 (Chapter 611, Stats. 1985) regarding Statements of Economic Interest, particularly regarding whether an assuming office statement or annual statement is required in 1986 and when the first filing is due?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The County Counsel is correct that no assuming office or annual statement is required in 1986. The first filing required is the annual Form 721 (not Form 730) due April 1, 1987, covering calendar year 1986, filed through the county Clerk.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains three components: (1) January 28, 1986 FPPC advice letter from Robert E. Leidigh responding to the County Counsel's request, (2) December 19, 1985 FPPC technical assistance memo to city and county attorneys, and (3) January 10, 1986 incoming request letter from County Counsel Westman. The primary advice letter response is the first component. Minor signature illegibility noted but content is clear.

QUESTION: You have written requesting our advice regarding your filing obligations under the recently revised provisions of Government code section 87200, et seg., amended by Chapter 611, Stats. 1985. Specifically, whether as County Counsel assuming a four-year term in 1984 and not anticipating leaving office during 1986, you are required to file an "Assuming Office Statement of Economic Interest" or "Annual Statement" during 1986, and when your first filing with the Commission will be due.

CONCLUSION: Your understanding of your requirements is correct. For calendar year 1985, you will continue to file a Form 730 pursuant to your agency's Conflict of Interest Code. For calendar year 1986, you will file pursuant to the requirements of Section 87200, et seg. This means that your Statement of Economic Interests for 1986 will be filed with our office by the county Clerk after you have filed the Form 721 with that office. That statement will be due in the Spring of 1987.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to County Counsel Victor Westman's inquiry regarding filing obligations under the newly amended Government Code section 87200 (Chapter 611, Stats. 1985). The letter confirms that for 1985 the County Counsel must file Form 730 under the local conflict of interest code, but for 1986 and forward must file Form 721 with the FPPC, with the first annual filing due April 1, 1987.
